Coming up next (all times IST):

Argentina v Austria — 22 Jun, 10:30 PM IST (Group J)

France v Iraq — 23 Jun, 02:30 AM IST (Group I)

Norway v Senegal — 23 Jun, 05:30 AM IST (Group I)

Jordan v Algeria — 23 Jun, 08:30 AM IST (Group J)

Full group standings, scorers and the complete fixture list are on our FIFA World Cup 2026 page, updated through the day.
key_facts: Belgium 0-0 Iran
New Zealand 1-3 Egypt
Uruguay 2-2 Cape Verde
Germany 2-1 Ivory Coast

stories.headline: Marizanne Kapp stars as South Africa beat India to keep Women's T20 World Cup hopes alive
stories.what: South Africa defeated India in their Women's T20 World Cup 2026 group match, handing India their first loss of the tournament. Marizanne Kapp was the standout performer for South Africa, driving the win that keeps South Africa's semifinal hopes alive. The result complicates India's path through the group stage and sets up a tense final round of fixtures. It is a significant upset given India had been unbeaten heading into the game.
stories.lens: India's Women's T20 World Cup semifinal spot is now uncertain — a tournament India were expected to top their group in — making the remaining fixtures high-stakes for millions of fans.
stories.article:
South Africa ended India's unbeaten run at the Women's T20 World Cup 2026, beating them in a group-stage match that shifts the semifinal picture for both sides. Marizanne Kapp was the architect of South Africa's victory, delivering a match-winning performance that gave her team a lifeline in the tournament.

India had entered the fixture as the stronger side on paper, having won their earlier group games. But South Africa produced a disciplined and clinical display, with Kapp's contribution — whether with bat, ball, or both — proving the decisive difference. The defeat is India's first of the tournament and comes at a critical juncture in the group stage.

For South Africa, the win is more than a confidence boost — it keeps their semifinal qualification bid firmly alive. A loss would have effectively ended their campaign, making this a must-win that they delivered on under pressure. The result injects fresh uncertainty into what had looked like a straightforward group for India.

India now need to reassess their qualification arithmetic. Depending on other results in the group, they may need to win their remaining match and hope other fixtures fall their way. The loss also serves as a warning that the Indian women's side cannot afford complacency in a competitive tournament field.

The match underlines South Africa's growing strength in women's cricket, with Kapp — one of the most experienced and dangerous all-rounders in the game — reminding everyone why she remains a match-winner on the biggest stages.
key_facts: South Africa beat India — India's first loss of Women's T20 World Cup 2026
Marizanne Kapp was South Africa's standout performer
Win keeps South Africa's semifinal qualification hopes alive
India had been unbeaten in the tournament before this match

stories.headline: Sloppy India lose Manchester thriller to South Africa, now in danger of early Women's T20 World Cup exit
stories.what: India Women suffered their first defeat of the Women's T20 World Cup 2026, going down to South Africa in a thrilling match in Manchester. Marizanne Kapp was the standout performer for South Africa, whose victory keeps their own tournament hopes alive. The loss puts India in danger of an early exit from the competition, a major setback for a side that had started the tournament in promising form.
stories.lens: India's Women's T20 World Cup campaign, which had looked steady, is now suddenly precarious — a further slip could mean an early exit from a marquee global tournament on foreign soil.
stories.article:
India Women's campaign at the 2026 Women's T20 World Cup hit a serious roadblock on Saturday night as a sloppy batting and fielding display handed South Africa a tense victory in Manchester, leaving the Indian side in real danger of an early tournament exit.

Marizanne Kapp was the match-winner for South Africa, delivering a match-turning performance — with both bat and ball, as she is known to do — to keep her side's hopes of progressing alive. India, who had entered the fixture with a degree of confidence, were undone by a combination of their own errors and South Africa's clinical execution in the crunch moments.

The defeat is India's first in the tournament and shifts the pressure squarely onto their remaining group-stage fixtures. A side that cannot afford another stumble, India will need to regroup quickly and find the form that had earlier defined their campaign. The result was played out at what India Today described as a 'Manchester thriller', suggesting the match went deep before South Africa sealed it.

For South Africa, the win is a lifeline, reviving their qualification prospects and underlining the quality of their experienced players in high-pressure situations. Kapp's starring role will give their camp a significant confidence boost heading into the business end of the group stage.

India's management and players will now face intense scrutiny. The coming days will determine whether this defeat proves a wake-up call or the beginning of a disappointing early departure from a tournament India had targeted as a major prize.
key_facts: India Women's first defeat of the Women's T20 World Cup 2026
South Africa won the match in Manchester
Marizanne Kapp was South Africa's standout performer
India now in danger of early tournament exit

stories.headline: India vs Ireland T20 Series 2026: Full schedule, match dates, venues and timings announced
stories.what: The schedule for the India vs Ireland T20 International series in 2026 has been announced, with match dates, venues and start times now confirmed. The series gives the Indian T20 squad further competitive opportunities in what is a busy international calendar. Details of the venues and timings allow fans to plan ahead for what will be eagerly followed fixtures. The Indian Express has published the full breakdown of the schedule for fans and followers of the team.
stories.lens: For Indian cricket fans planning to watch or attend matches, having the confirmed IND vs IRE T20 series schedule — dates, venues and times — is practical, useful information.
stories.article:
The schedule for the India versus Ireland T20 International series in 2026 has been confirmed, with the Indian Express publishing the full details of match dates, venues and start times for fans to plan around.

The series forms part of a packed international calendar for the Indian T20 side, offering the team a competitive set of fixtures. Ireland, while not among the top-ranked T20 sides, have shown the ability to compete and spring surprises in the shortest format of the game, making the series a useful exercise for the Indian squad.

For the Board of Control for Cricket in India and its counterpart Cricket Ireland, the series also carries commercial and developmental significance — providing Ireland with high-profile exposure and India with warm-up opportunities ahead of bigger tournaments.

Fans looking to attend matches or follow the series on broadcast will now be able to lock in the dates. The venues chosen will also generate local interest, with host cities gearing up for the arrival of the Indian team.

Full scheduling details, including exact timings adjusted for Indian Standard Time, are available via the Indian Express report for those wanting to follow every ball of the series.
key_facts: India vs Ireland T20 series 2026 schedule officially announced
Match dates, venues and start times confirmed
Series part of India's busy 2026 international T20 calendar

stories.headline: How India can still qualify for Women's T20 World Cup semis after South Africa loss
stories.what: Following India's defeat to South Africa at the Women's T20 World Cup 2026, the qualification scenarios for the semifinal round have become more complex for the Indian women's team. India are no longer guaranteed a top-two finish in their group and must now rely on winning their remaining fixture as well as other results going their way. The Indian Express has broken down the exact permutations India need to advance, making this a critical watch for fans ahead of the final group-stage round.
stories.lens: Indian fans tracking the Women's T20 World Cup now face a genuine knockout anxiety — understanding the qualification math is essential as India's semifinal berth is no longer a given.
stories.article:
India's Women's T20 World Cup 2026 semifinal qualification is no longer straightforward after their loss to South Africa, and the focus has quickly shifted to what combinations of results can still see the team through to the last four.

India must win their remaining group-stage fixture — that much is clear. But whether that is sufficient depends on how other matches in the group play out, including what South Africa do in their final game. Net run rate could also come into play if teams finish level on points, adding another layer of pressure to India's remaining match.

The qualification picture is not hopeless. India still hold their fate partially in their own hands, and a convincing win in their next game would go a long way toward securing progress. The team's batting and bowling units will need to fire together to not only win but potentially improve their run rate as insurance.

The scenario is a familiar one in tournament cricket — a single slip can turn a comfortable qualification path into a nervy final-round watch. For the Indian women's side, which had been in commanding form before the South Africa match, it is a test of composure and execution under pressure.

Coach and team management will be focused on keeping the squad settled and not letting the qualification arithmetic become a distraction. The priority, as always, is to play their best cricket and let the results follow.
key_facts: India must win remaining group game to stay in contention
Net run rate may become a factor if teams finish level on points
India's semifinal path now depends partly on other group results
South Africa's win created the qualification uncertainty

stories.headline: Did Amitabh Bachchan shelve Khabardar to avoid being overshadowed by Kamal Haasan?
stories.what: A long-circulating piece of Bollywood lore has resurfaced: that Amitabh Bachchan pulled out of — or effectively shelved — the film Khabardar because he feared being outshone by co-star Kamal Haasan. The Indian Express revisits the story behind the ill-fated collaboration between two of Hindi cinema's biggest names, a project that was never completed. The episode, if true, would represent a rare instance of Bollywood's biggest superstar stepping back from a project for fear of artistic competition.
stories.lens: The Bachchan-Haasan rivalry anecdote is a window into the ego politics and star-power calculations that have always shaped — and sometimes killed — ambitious Bollywood projects.
stories.article:
Decades after the fact, one of Bollywood's most tantalising 'what-ifs' is being revisited: did Amitabh Bachchan effectively kill the film Khabardar because he feared being outperformed by Kamal Haasan? The Indian Express digs into the rumour that has persisted in film circles for years, examining the circumstances that led to the project being shelved.

The story, as it has circulated in Bollywood lore, goes that Bachchan — then at the peak of his 'angry young man' superstardom — grew uneasy about sharing screen space with Kamal Haasan, widely regarded as one of the most technically gifted actors of his generation. Haasan's ability to transform physically and emotionally for roles was already becoming legendary, and the concern, reportedly, was that audiences might walk away talking more about him than the top-billed Bachchan.

Such dynamics were not uncommon in the Hindi film industry of that era. The star system placed enormous importance on hierarchy — who got more screen time, whose name appeared first, and crucially, who audiences went home remembering. A multi-starrer that upset that calculus could be commercially and reputationally damaging for the bigger name. Whether Bachchan's team made a deliberate call or circumstances conspired is something the report examines without definitive conclusion.

What makes the anecdote enduring is what it says about two parallel legends: Bachchan, the undisputed commercial king of Hindi cinema, and Haasan, the actor's actor who rarely played by Bollywood's star-system rules. A completed Khabardar would have been one of the most fascinating screen pairings of that era — and its absence remains one of Indian cinema's great unmade films.
key_facts: Film Khabardar was reportedly shelved after Amitabh Bachchan's exit
Kamal Haasan was co-star; Bachchan allegedly feared being overshadowed
Project represents one of Bollywood's most notable 'unmade films'

stories.headline: How Om Puri's craft silenced Raaj Kumar's on-set insults
stories.what: A behind-the-scenes anecdote about the late actors Om Puri and Raaj Kumar has resurfaced, describing how Puri's raw acting ability effectively stopped Raaj Kumar — notoriously imperious on film sets — from directing his trademark contempt at him. The story, revisited by The Indian Express, illustrates the complex hierarchies and personality clashes that defined Hindi cinema's golden era. It also serves as a tribute to Om Puri's formidable reputation as one of the most technically accomplished actors of his generation.
stories.lens: For fans of classic Hindi cinema, this anecdote captures something real about how genuine craft — not stardom — was the ultimate currency even in an industry driven by ego and hierarchy.
stories.article:
On the sets of Hindi cinema's golden era, few personalities were as intimidating as Raaj Kumar — an actor famed as much for his imperious manner and sharp tongue as for his screen presence. A story now doing the rounds, revisited by The Indian Express, describes how the late Om Puri managed to do what few could: render Raaj Kumar's disdain utterly ineffective, simply through the power of his acting.

The anecdote captures a moment where Raaj Kumar, known for belittling co-stars and crew, found himself unable to sustain his usual condescension in the face of Puri's extraordinary craft. Puri — trained at the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII) and the National School of Drama — brought an intensity to his performances that was virtually impossible to dismiss or diminish, even for someone of Raaj Kumar's formidable ego.

Om Puri's career was built on exactly this kind of quiet authority. From Ardh Satya to Jaane Bhi Do Yaaron, he repeatedly demonstrated that pure acting technique could command a room and a camera in ways that star power alone could not. The fact that even Raaj Kumar — no shrinking violet himself — had to concede that space is a telling measure of Puri's stature.

Both actors are now gone, but stories like these keep their legacies alive in vivid, human terms. The anecdote is less about rivalry and more about the unspoken grammar of respect on a film set — that ultimately, craft speaks loudest.
key_facts: Om Puri trained at FTII and National School of Drama
Raaj Kumar was known for his imperious, often dismissive manner on sets
Both actors are now deceased; anecdote revisits Hindi cinema's golden era

stories.headline: Main Vaapas Aaunga earns Rs 41 crore in 10 days, collects 5x its opening day
stories.what: Imtiaz Ali's Main Vaapas Aaunga has collected Rs 41 crore at the box office by its tenth day of release, having grown to five times its opening day collection. The film's steady climb signals strong word-of-mouth momentum rather than a front-loaded debut. The performance marks a notable mid-scale success in a competitive theatrical window.
stories.lens: For Bollywood watchers, Main Vaapas Aaunga's 5x multiplier over its opening day is a textbook example of a film that built its audience through word of mouth rather than a blockbuster first weekend — a rarer and arguably healthier box-office trajectory.
stories.article:
Imtiaz Ali's Main Vaapas Aaunga has crossed Rs 41 crore at the box office by its tenth day in cinemas, having earned five times what it made on its opening day — a trajectory that studios and distributors consider a strong indicator of genuine audience approval.

The multiplier of 5x over the opening day figure is particularly significant in an era when big-budget Hindi films typically front-load their earnings on opening weekends driven by paid previews and fan shows. Main Vaapas Aaunga's curve runs in the opposite direction, suggesting that early audiences recommended it widely enough to pull in new viewers well into its second week.

The Rs 41 crore total, reported by The Indian Express on day ten, places the film comfortably in profitable territory for a mid-budget Bollywood release. Imtiaz Ali, known for films like Jab We Met, Love Aaj Kal and Tamasha, has had an uneven box-office record in recent years, making this sustained run a meaningful rebound for the director.

With the second weekend still playing out, trade analysts will be watching whether the film can extend its run further or whether the curve flattens. Either way, the day-ten number already makes Main Vaapas Aaunga one of the steadier Hindi theatrical performers of 2025 so far.
key_facts: Rs 41 crore collected by day 10
Box office earnings are 5x the opening day figure
Directed by Imtiaz Ali

stories.headline: Sona Mohapatra says Bollywood heartbreak songs are 'reserved for men', questions erasure of women's pain
stories.what: Singer Sona Mohapatra has publicly called out Bollywood's songwriting culture, arguing that heartbreak and longing in Hindi film music are almost exclusively portrayed from a male perspective. She questioned why women's emotional experiences are so rarely centred in mainstream compositions, asking pointedly whether men are effectively writing songs about their feelings for themselves. The comment has sparked fresh debate about gender representation in the Hindi film music industry.
stories.lens: Sona Mohapatra's critique goes to the heart of whose stories get told in Bollywood's biggest emotional format — the film song — and why millions of Indian women find so few anthems that actually speak to their own heartbreaks.
stories.article:
Singer and activist Sona Mohapatra has taken aim at the emotional economy of Bollywood music, arguing that songs about heartbreak and romantic longing in Hindi cinema are almost entirely written from and for a male gaze, effectively erasing women's pain from one of popular culture's most consumed formats.

Mohapatra framed her critique sharply, asking whether men in Bollywood are essentially making love to themselves — writing, composing, and performing songs about their own grief while the woman in the narrative remains a passive object of longing rather than a subject with her own emotional interiority. The comment, reported by The Times of India, is pointed: in a typical Bollywood separation arc, it is the male protagonist who mourns, yearns and expresses, while the woman either disappears or becomes the cause of suffering rather than a sufferer herself.

The observation connects to a broader, ongoing conversation about representation in Hindi film industries — who writes the scripts, who composes the music, and whose emotional vocabulary ends up defining mainstream culture. Women have historically been underrepresented among Bollywood's top lyricists and composers, a structural gap that arguably shapes what kinds of feelings get recorded and amplified.

Mohapatra has a history of public commentary on the music industry, including on issues of credit, pay and creative autonomy for women artists. Her latest remarks are likely to resonate with listeners who have long noted the asymmetry — and to push back from those who argue the industry is changing.
key_facts: Mohapatra says Bollywood heartbreak songs centre male emotional experience
Questions why women's romantic pain is absent from mainstream Hindi music
Comment reported by The Times of India

stories.headline: Why Agent Vinod remains Sriram Raghavan's most fascinating failure, over a decade on
stories.what: The Indian Express has revisited Sriram Raghavan's 2012 spy thriller Agent Vinod, starring Saif Ali Khan, examining why the film — despite its ambition, globe-trotting scale and the director's signature stylistic flair — failed to connect with audiences and critics on release. The piece argues that the film's flaws are as interesting as its achievements, making it a compelling case study in big-budget Hindi filmmaking gone sideways. The reassessment comes as Raghavan's reputation, burnished by Andhadhun, continues to grow.
stories.lens: For cinephiles, the Agent Vinod reassessment is a reminder that Sriram Raghavan — now regarded as one of Hindi cinema's finest thriller directors — built that reputation partly through a film that flopped, and that failure in Bollywood can be as instructive as success.
stories.article:
More than a decade after its release, Agent Vinod — Sriram Raghavan's ambitious 2012 spy thriller starring Saif Ali Khan — is getting a fresh critical look, with The Indian Express arguing that the film remains the director's most fascinating failure: a project that reached for something genuinely different in Hindi commercial cinema and came up short in ways that are worth examining.

Raghavan, who had previously made Johnny Gaddaar and would go on to direct the widely acclaimed Andhadhun, conceived Agent Vinod as a big-budget homage to classic Cold War spy cinema — think less Salman Khan action and more John le Carré filtered through Bollywood glamour. Shot across multiple countries, the film had an unusually sprawling canvas for a Hindi production of its era. Yet on release, it was met with mixed reviews and underperformed commercially, leaving behind an odd legacy: beloved by a small but vocal subset of Hindi film enthusiasts, largely forgotten by the mainstream.

The reassessment positions the film's failure not as straightforward incompetence but as a symptom of the structural tensions Raghavan was navigating — between the demands of a star vehicle, the expectations of a mass entertainer, and his own instincts as a genre craftsman. The film tried to do too many things at once, the argument goes, and its messiness is inseparable from its genuine moments of invention.

With Raghavan's reputation now at a high point, the piece serves as a reminder that even the most acclaimed directors in Indian cinema have complicated roads behind them — and that revisiting those detours can illuminate both the artist and the industry.
key_facts: Agent Vinod released in 2012, directed by Sriram Raghavan
Starred Saif Ali Khan; underperformed commercially on release
Raghavan later directed acclaimed Andhadhun (2018)
